title: Gin Is Torn Between Krieg and Sanji
description: On pages 14–17, the fight against Krieg reaches a moral breaking point as Gin, Sanji, Zeff, and Luffy clash over debt, survival, and strength.
scene: Krieg’s weapons and cruelty force everyone at the Baratie to reveal what they truly value: victory, loyalty, survival, or protecting someone else’s dream.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You won't be repaying any debt by dying!!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That's not what he saved you for!

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: to throw away your life and die like this after being saved...

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: Well, it's not like you know of a better way out of this mess!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Now, now. Stop the fighting, you two.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: It's just your bad fortune to have gone up against the Krieg Pirates.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: There's nothin' you can do against us!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: As long as we have a hostage, that is!

dialogue.speaker: Pearl
dialogue.text: Sorry Pearl, but I need you to get out of my way for a little while.

summary: This part pushes the Baratie battle toward its climax by contrasting Krieg’s ruthless power with Luffy and Sanji’s stubborn ideals.
